content.text: FastAPI is the standard choice for Python GenAI backends. It is fully async (built on Starlette and asyncio), auto-generates OpenAPI docs, and validates request/response bodies with Pydantic — essential for LLM APIs where the input schema (messages, parameters, system prompts) must be strict but the output is text.
The core challenge is that LLM calls are slow (seconds) and often best served as streams. FastAPI's
route handlers make both straightforward without blocking workers.

attrs.alt: FastAPI GenAI backend request flow with middleware, LLM client, and side services

content.text: Every request passes through the middleware stack (CORS, auth, rate limiting, tracing) before reaching the route handler. The handler validates the body with Pydantic, builds the prompt, calls the LLM provider asynchronously, and streams the response back via SSE. Redis, Postgres, and a vector DB serve as side services for rate limiting, conversation history, and RAG retrieval.

app/
├── main.py            # FastAPI app, middleware, lifespan
├── api/
│   ├── chat.py        # POST /chat/completions  (streaming)
│   ├── health.py      # GET /health
│   └── deps.py        # FastAPI dependency functions
├── models/
│   └── schemas.py     # Pydantic request/response models
├── services/
│   ├── llm.py         # LLM client wrapper (Anthropic / OpenAI)
│   └── history.py     # conversation history store
└── config.py          # settings from env vars
Define strict input/output schemas with Pydantic v2. FastAPI validates these automatically and surfaces errors as 422 responses before the LLM is ever called:
with an async generator to forward SSE chunks from the LLM to the client. The event loop never blocks — each
yields control while waiting for the next token:

# app/api/chat.py
import json, uuid
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse
from app.models.schemas import ChatRequest
from app.api.deps import get_current_user, get_rate_limiter
from app.services.llm import stream_llm, complete_llm

router = APIRouter(prefix="/chat", tags=["chat"])

async def sse_generator(request: ChatRequest):
    """Yield SSE-formatted chunks from the LLM."""
    async for chunk in stream_llm(request):
        data = json.dumps({"delta": chunk})
        yield f"data: {data}\n\n"
    yield "data: [DONE]\n\n"

@router.post("/completions")
async def chat_completions(
    request: ChatRequest,
    user=Depends(get_current_user),
    _=Depends(get_rate_limiter),
):
    if request.stream:
        return StreamingResponse(
            sse_generator(request),
            media_type="text/event-stream",
            headers={
                "Cache-Control": "no-cache",
                "X-Accel-Buffering": "no",  # disable nginx buffering
            },
        )
    response = await complete_llm(request)
    return response
Wrap the provider SDK in a service that normalises differences between Anthropic and OpenAI and handles retries:

# app/services/llm.py
import anthropic
from app.models.schemas import ChatRequest
from app.config import settings

_client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(api_key=settings.anthropic_api_key)

async def stream_llm(request: ChatRequest):
    """Async generator: yields text deltas from the LLM."""
    # Separate system message from the conversation messages
    system = next(
        (m.content for m in request.messages if m.role == "system"),
        "You are a helpful assistant.",
    )
    messages = [
        {"role": m.role, "content": m.content}
        for m in request.messages
        if m.role != "system"
    ]

    async with _client.messages.stream(
        model=request.model,
        max_tokens=request.max_tokens,
        temperature=request.temperature,
        system=system,
        messages=messages,
    ) as stream:
        async for text in stream.text_stream:
            yield text

async def complete_llm(request: ChatRequest) -> dict:
    """Non-streaming call; returns complete response."""
    system = next(
        (m.content for m in request.messages if m.role == "system"),
        "You are a helpful assistant.",
    )
    messages = [
        {"role": m.role, "content": m.content}
        for m in request.messages
        if m.role != "system"
    ]
    response = await _client.messages.create(
        model=request.model,
        max_tokens=request.max_tokens,
        temperature=request.temperature,
        system=system,
        messages=messages,
    )
    return {
        "id": response.id,
        "content": response.content[0].text,
        "model": response.model,
        "usage": {
            "input_tokens": response.usage.input_tokens,
            "output_tokens": response.usage.output_tokens,
        },
    }

# app/main.py
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from app.api import chat, health
from app.config import settings

@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    # Startup: initialise connection pools
    from app.services import db, redis_client
    await db.connect()
    await redis_client.connect()
    yield
    # Shutdown: close connections
    await db.disconnect()
    await redis_client.close()

app = FastAPI(
    title="GenAI API",
    version="1.0.0",
    lifespan=lifespan,
)

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=settings.allowed_origins,
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["POST", "GET"],
    allow_headers=["Authorization", "Content-Type"],
)

app.include_router(chat.router)
app.include_router(health.router)
Dependency Injection: Auth and Rate Limiting

# app/api/deps.py
import time
from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, status
from fastapi.security import HTTPBearer, HTTPAuthorizationCredentials
import jwt
from app.config import settings
from app.services import redis_client

security = HTTPBearer()

async def get_current_user(
    credentials: HTTPAuthorizationCredentials = Depends(security),
) -> dict:
    try:
        payload = jwt.decode(
            credentials.credentials,
            settings.jwt_secret,
            algorithms=["HS256"],
        )
        return payload
    except jwt.ExpiredSignatureError:
        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, "Token expired")
    except jwt.InvalidTokenError:
        raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, "Invalid token")

async def get_rate_limiter(user: dict = Depends(get_current_user)) -> None:
    user_id = user["sub"]
    key = f"rate:{user_id}"
    count = await redis_client.incr(key)
    if count == 1:
        await redis_client.expire(key, 60)   # 60-second window
    if count > settings.requests_per_minute:
        raise HTTPException(
            status.HTTP_429_TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,
            f"Rate limit: {settings.requests_per_minute} requests/minute",
        )
for readiness. The ready check verifies that all dependencies (LLM API key, Redis, Postgres) are reachable:

# app/config.py
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings

class Settings(BaseSettings):
    anthropic_api_key: str
    jwt_secret: str
    database_url: str
    redis_url: str = "redis://localhost:6379"
    allowed_origins: list[str] = ["http://localhost:3000"]
    requests_per_minute: int = 20
    environment: str = "development"

    class Config:
        env_file = ".env"

settings = Settings()

# .env (never commit this file)
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
JWT_SECRET=a-random-256-bit-secret
DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://user:pass@localhost/genai
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379

# Development
uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8000

# Production (multiple workers)
uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --workers 4

# Or with gunicorn (recommended for production):
gunicorn app.main:app \
    --worker-class uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker \
    --workers 4 \
    --bind 0.0.0.0:8000
if the app holds in-memory state (e.g. a model loaded into GPU RAM). Multiple workers work correctly when all shared state lives in Redis or Postgres.

# tests/test_chat.py
import pytest
from httpx import AsyncClient, ASGITransport
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch
from app.main import app

@pytest.fixture
def auth_headers():
    import jwt
    from app.config import settings
    token = jwt.encode({"sub": "user-123"}, settings.jwt_secret, algorithm="HS256")
    return {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_chat_streaming(auth_headers):
    async def fake_stream(_request):
        for chunk in ["Hello", ", ", "world", "!"]:
            yield chunk

    with patch("app.services.llm.stream_llm", side_effect=fake_stream):
        with patch("app.api.deps.redis_client") as mock_redis:
            mock_redis.incr = AsyncMock(return_value=1)
            mock_redis.expire = AsyncMock()
            async with AsyncClient(
                transport=ASGITransport(app=app), base_url="http://test"
            ) as client:
                response = await client.post(
                    "/chat/completions",
                    json={"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hi"}]},
                    headers=auth_headers,
                )
                assert response.status_code == 200
                assert "text/event-stream" in response.headers["content-type"]
                body = response.text
                assert '"delta": "Hello"' in body
                assert "[DONE]" in body
Blocking the event loop with sync LLM calls:
(sync client) inside an async route blocks every other request. Always use
nginx buffers SSE by default, so the client sees nothing until the full response is complete. Add this header to the StreamingResponse.
Catching all exceptions at the route level:
that returns 200 hides LLM errors. Let FastAPI's exception handlers return 500 with a structured error body.
Leaking the API key in error messages:
in log output or error responses.
Using workers > 1 with in-process GPU models:
loading a model in each worker multiplies VRAM use. Use workers=1 and async concurrency, or offload the model to a separate serving process.
FastAPI's async model and Pydantic validation make it the right foundation for GenAI backends. Use
to keep the event loop free, serve streaming responses with
and an async generator, and inject auth + rate limiting as FastAPI dependencies so the route handler stays focused on LLM logic.
